web.title: FormForge API — Render HTML Forms from JSON
web.description: How does FormForge generate forms? You send a JSON schema defining your form fields, types, and validation rules. FormForge returns production-ready HTML ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/#main)

# JSON in. Beautiful forms out.  _One API call._

Send a JSON form definition and get back a fully styled, accessible HTML form with client-side validation — ready to embed anywhere.

API responding in 1588ms

**10** field types**4** built-in themes**20** free forms/day**Zero** dependencies

```
# Render a contact form with client-side validation
curl -X POST https://formforge-api.vercel.app/api/json-to-form \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "title": "Contact Us",
    "theme": "modern",
    "submitUrl": "https://example.com/submit",
    "fields": [\
      { "name": "name",    "type": "text",     "label": "Full Name",  "required": true },\
      { "name": "email",   "type": "email",    "label": "Email",      "required": true },\
      { "name": "message", "type": "textarea", "label": "Message" }\
    ]
  }'

# Returns: { "html": "<!DOCTYPE html>...", "meta": { "theme": "modern", ... } }
```

## Ten field types, no fuss

Everything you need to build any form or survey.

text

Single-line free text

email

Email with format validation

number

Numeric input with min/max

textarea

Multi-line free text

select

Dropdown from options list

checkbox

Boolean toggle or multi-select

radio

Single choice from options

date

Date picker

tel

Phone number with validation

url

URL with format validation

## Four themes. All beautiful.

Choose the theme that fits your product — or embed the HTML and override any CSS variable.

Email \*

your@email.com

Message

Submit

modern — clean & minimal


Email \*

your@email.com

Message

Submit

corporate — professional blue


Email \*

your@email.com

Message

Submit

playful — rounded & colorful


Email \*

your@email.com

Message

Submit

minimal — borderless & spacious


## How it works

1

### Define your fields

Describe your form fields in JSON — name, type, label, validation rules, and options.

2

### Call the API

POST your schema to `/api/json-to-form` or `/api/json-to-embed` with your API key.

3

### Embed the HTML

Use the returned HTML directly in an <iframe> or inject it into your page. No extra CSS needed.

## Try it live

Edit the JSON on the left, click Render, and see your form appear instantly.

Contact formCustomer surveyEvent registrationProduct feedbackmoderncorporateplayfulminimalRender FormCopy HTML

JSON Schema

We'd love to hear from you.

Your form was submitted successfully. Thank you!

Subject \*Select Subject…General enquirySupportSalesPartnership

I agree to be contacted about my enquiry

Send Message

## Try it now

Paste a JSON form definition and hit Generate — no signup required.

Generate FormReset

JSON Input

Result

## Simple, honest pricing

### Free

$0

forever

- 20 forms per day
- 50 KB input limit
- All 10 field types
- All 4 themes
- No credit card needed

[Get free key](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/#signup)

### Builder

$14

per month

- 300 forms per day
- 512 KB input limit
- All field types & themes
- Rate-limit headers
- Email support

[Start building](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/#signup)

### Enterprise

$39

per month

- Unlimited forms
- 5 MB input limit
- All field types & themes
- Priority support
- SLA guarantee

[Contact us](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/#signup)

## Why FormForge?

### Zero dependencies

Every form is a single self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and vanilla JS. No React, no jQuery, no CDN links. It just works.

### Accessible by default

Every field gets proper `aria-*` attributes, role labels, and live error regions. Screen-reader friendly out of the box.

### Validation included

Client-side validation for required fields, email format, URL format, phone numbers, number ranges, and text length — no extra JS needed.

### Embed anywhere

Drop into an <iframe>, inject with `innerHTML`, or serve directly. The HTML is fully self-contained.

### Async submit ready

Pass a `submitUrl` and the form handles the fetch call, loading state, success, and error banners automatically.

### REST API, no SDK

One POST request with a JSON body. Works from any language, framework, or plain `curl`. No SDK installation needed.

## Get your free API key

20 forms per day, no credit card required.

Get Key

We'll email your API key. No spam — ever.

Your API key is ready!

Save this key — it won't be shown again. Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_KEY`

## Frequently Asked Questions

How does FormForge generate forms?

You send a JSON schema defining your form fields, types, and validation rules. FormForge returns production-ready HTML with built-in styling, validation, and accessibility attributes.

Are the generated forms accessible?

Yes, all forms include proper ARIA labels, roles, error announcements, keyboard navigation, and focus management. Forms meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards out of the box.

Can I style the generated forms?

Yes, generated forms use semantic HTML with CSS classes you can override. You can also pass a custom CSS URL in the API request to apply your own theme.

What field types are supported?

FormForge supports text, email, password, number, date, select, checkbox, radio, textarea, file upload, and range inputs. Compound types like address and phone are also available.

Is there a free tier?

Yes, FormForge is free for up to 30 forms per day. Builder ($14/mo) offers 3,000 forms/day, and Enterprise ($39/mo) offers unlimited forms with priority support and custom themes.

## Trusted by developers

★★★★★

"We ship forms 10x faster. What used to require a frontend sprint now takes a single API call."

Rachel Torres

Engineering Manager at FormStack

★★★★★

"The ARIA compliance is built-in, which means we pass accessibility audits without extra work. Game changer."

Kevin Okafor

Accessibility Engineer at A11yFirst

★★★★★

"Our no-code platform uses FormForge under the hood. Customers define forms in JSON and we render them instantly."

Nina Vasquez

Co-founder at BuildFlow

We use cookies and analytics to improve your experience. By continuing to use this site, you agree to our [Privacy Policy](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/privacy).

[View commit history for this file.](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/commits/main/) 3 Commits | | [README.md](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [README.md](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/blob/main/README.md "README.md") | [Update README with comprehensive documentation and badges](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/commit/63505d379f5da9d7f467deedfafc80cc9e7d62c5 "Update README with comprehensive documentation and badges") | 3 months agoFeb 28, 2026 | | View all files | ## Repository files navigation # FormForge API [Permalink: FormForge API](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#formforge-api) [![API Status](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/7702853ef3edec09a46577a126b62288e31f1744daf0d7d49f75719d1a9f5814/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f4150492d4c6976652d627269676874677265656e)](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/)[![License: MIT](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/08cef40a9105b6526ca22088bc514fbfdbc9aac1ddbf8d4e6c750e3a88a44dca/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f4c6963656e73652d4d49542d626c75652e737667)](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/blob/main/LICENSE) Convert JSON form schemas into styled, accessible HTML forms with a simple REST API. [Live Site](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/)  ·  [API Docs](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/docs)  ·  [Blog](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/blog)  ·  [Status](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/status) * * * ## Features [Permalink: Features](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#features) - **3 Themes** — Modern, Corporate, and Playful styles ready to embed - **10 Field Types** — text, email, number, textarea, select, checkbox, radio, date, tel, url - **Client-Side Validation** — Required fields, email format, number ranges, pattern matching - **ARIA Accessible** — Semantic labels, keyboard navigation, screen reader support - **Responsive Design** — Mobile-first, adapts to any container width - **Free Tier** — 20 forms per day at zero cost, no credit card required * * * ## Quick Start [Permalink: Quick Start](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#quick-start) ### 1\. Get Your API Key [Permalink: 1. Get Your API Key](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#1-get-your-api-key) ``` curl -X POST https://formforge-api.vercel.app/api/signup \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"email": "you@example.com"}' ``` ### 2\. Generate a Form [Permalink: 2. Generate a Form](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#2-generate-a-form) ``` curl -X POST https://formforge-api.vercel.app/api/json-to-form \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "title": "Contact Us", "theme": "modern", "fields": [\ {"name": "name", "type": "text", "label": "Full Name", "required": true},\ {"name": "email", "type": "email", "label": "Email Address", "required": true},\ {"name": "message", "type": "textarea", "label": "Message", "required": true},\ {"name": "subscribe", "type": "checkbox", "label": "Subscribe to newsletter"}\ ] }' ``` ### 3\. Embed the HTML [Permalink: 3. Embed the HTML](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#3-embed-the-html) Paste the returned HTML into your page — no external scripts or stylesheets needed. ```
``` * * * ## API Endpoints [Permalink: API Endpoints](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#api-endpoints) | Method | Endpoint | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `POST` | `/api/signup` | Create a free-tier API key | | `POST` | `/api/json-to-form` | Generate an HTML form from a JSON schema | * * * ## Example Request & Response [Permalink: Example Request & Response](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#example-request--response) **Request** ``` curl -X POST https://formforge-api.vercel.app/api/json-to-form \ -H "Authorization: Bearer ff_abc123" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "title": "Contact Us", "theme": "modern", "fields": [\ {"name": "name", "type": "text", "label": "Full Name", "required": true},\ {"name": "email", "type": "email", "label": "Email Address", "required": true},\ {"name": "message", "type": "textarea", "label": "Message", "required": true},\ {"name": "subscribe", "type": "checkbox", "label": "Subscribe to newsletter"}\ ] }' ``` **Response** (`200 OK`) ``` { "ok": true, "html": "...", "usage": { "used": 1, "limit": 20, "remaining": 19 } } ``` The `html` field contains a complete, self-contained HTML document with inline CSS and JavaScript — ready to embed or serve as a standalone page. * * * ## Supported Field Types [Permalink: Supported Field Types](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#supported-field-types) | Type | Description | Validation | | --- | --- | --- | | `text` | Single-line text input | Required, min/max length | | `email` | Email address input | Format validation | | `number` | Numeric input | Min/max value, step | | `textarea` | Multi-line text input | Required, min/max length | | `select` | Dropdown menu | Required selection | | `checkbox` | Single checkbox toggle | Required acceptance | | `radio` | Radio button group | Required selection | | `date` | Date picker | Min/max date | | `tel` | Telephone number input | Pattern matching | | `url` | URL input | Format validation | * * * ## Themes [Permalink: Themes](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#themes) | Theme | Style | Colors | | --- | --- | --- | | **Modern** | Clean, minimal design with sharp edges | Emerald accents, white background | | **Corporate** | Professional layout with structured spacing | Navy blue, light gray tones | | **Playful** | Rounded corners, friendly feel | Purple gradients, soft shadows | Set the theme in your request body: ``` { "theme": "modern" } ``` * * * ## Pricing [Permalink: Pricing](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#pricing) | Tier | Rate Limit | Price | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Free** | 20 forms / day | $0 | Included with signup | | **Builder** | 300 forms / day | $14 / month | [Upgrade](https://buy.stripe.com/6oE14Mbt13qE2kw5kl) | | **Enterprise** | Unlimited | $39 / month | [Upgrade](https://buy.stripe.com/28o8xebt11iw8IUbIK) | All tiers include every theme, all field types, and full validation support. * * * ## Links [Permalink: Links](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#links) - [Live Site](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/) - [API Documentation](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/docs) - [Blog](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/blog) - [Status Page](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/status) - [OpenAPI Spec](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/openapi.json) * * * ## Related Products [Permalink: Related Products](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#related-products) | Product | Description | Link | | --- | --- | --- | | **DocForge API** | Document format conversion (Markdown, CSV, JSON, TXT to HTML) | [docforge-api.vercel.app](https://docforge-api.vercel.app/) | | **ReportForge API** | Professional report generation from CSV/JSON data | [reportforge-api.vercel.app](https://reportforge-api.vercel.app/) | * * * ## License [Permalink: License](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#license) [MIT](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api/blob/main/LICENSE) ## About Free REST API for generating styled, accessible HTML forms from JSON definitions. 4 themes, 10 field types, WCAG compliant. [formforge-api.vercel.app](https://formforge-api.vercel.app/ "https://formforge-api.vercel.app") ### Topics [api](https://github.com/topics/api "Topic: api") [typescript](https://github.com/topics/typescript "Topic: typescript") [validation](https://github.com/topics/validation "Topic: validation") [serverless](https://github.com/topics/serverless "Topic: serverless") [accessibility](https://github.com/topics/accessibility "Topic: accessibility") [wcag](https://github.com/topics/wcag "Topic: wcag") [form-builder](https://github.com/topics/form-builder "Topic: form-builder") [ui-components](https://github.com/topics/ui-components "Topic: ui-components") [html-forms](https://github.com/topics/html-forms "Topic: html-forms") [json-to-html](https://github.com/topics/json-to-html "Topic: json-to-html") [vercel](https://github.com/topics/vercel "Topic: vercel") ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/AlberaMarc/formforge-api#readme-ov-file) ### Uh oh! web.title: Form's JSON | Documentation - FormEngine
web.description: You have the option to generate a JSON schema for your set of components used in the form. After generating the schema, you can connect it to your code editor ...
web.markdown:
[Skip to main content](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

[Introducing Workflow Engine, try for FREE workflowengine.io.](https://workflowengine.io/documentation?utm_source=formengine&utm_medium=topbanner)

On this page

Each form created in the form designer is JSON. With the designer, you can export a form to JSON, and you can import a form from JSON.

Let's take for example the form from [this section](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/getting-started/overview/#creating-a-simple-form) of the
documentation.

## Export form to JSON [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#export-form-to-json "Direct link to Export form to JSON")

To export a JSON form, go to the designer interface and click the blue menu button and select "Download".

![Form's JSON 01](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-01-9f3b293f3b565632615797677bb311db.png)

The form will be saved to a file with the extension `.json`.

## Get the form's JSON in React component [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#get-the-forms-json-in-react-component "Direct link to Get the form's JSON in React component")

If you need get the form's JSON inside the React component body, you can use
the [IFormBuilder](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/designer/interfaces/IFormBuilder/) ref:

```tsx
const FormBuilderApp = () => {
  const builderRef = useRef<IFormBuilder>()

  const setRef = (builder: IFormBuilder | null) => {
    if (builder) {
      builderRef.current = builder
      const formJSON = builder.formAsString
      console.log(formJSON)
    }
  }

  return <FormBuilder
    builderRef={setRef}
    {...otherProps}
  />
}
```

## Import form from JSON [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#import-form-from-json "Direct link to Import form from JSON")

To import a form in JSON format, select the blue menu button in the designer, and then select "Upload".

![Form's JSON 02](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-02-144d310e1e1599b8ea9d767853529fe6.png)

## Embedded JSON form editor [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#embedded-json-form-editor "Direct link to Embedded JSON form editor")

You can also edit the form data in JSON directly in the form designer. To do this, you need to click the "Edit JSON" button, as in the
screenshot below.

![img.png](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-03-e2685c49751a8139268aed2a2bd7848b.png)

This opens the built-in JSON form code editor. After you close the form by clicking the close button in the top right corner of the screen,
the JSON with the form code will be applied in the form designer.

![img_1.png](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-04-da54309f4879f03986e3082b50cf6118.png)

## JSON form description [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#json-form-description "Direct link to JSON form description")

Let's take a look at what a JSON form is, below is an example:

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  "actions": {
    "logValue": {
      "body": "  console.log('Name', e.data.name)",
      "params": {}
    }
  },
  "tooltipType": "RsTooltip",
  "errorType": "RsErrorMessage",
  "form": {
    "key": "Screen",
    "type": "Screen",
    "props": {},
    "children": [\
      {\
        "key": "name",\
        "type": "RsInput",\
        "props": {\
          "label": {\
            "value": "Name"\
          }\
        },\
        "schema": {\
          "validations": [\
            {\
              "key": "required"\
            },\
            {\
              "key": "min",\
              "args": {\
                "limit": 3\
              }\
            }\
          ]\
        }\
      },\
      {\
        "key": "RsButton 1",\
        "type": "RsButton",\
        "props": {\
          "children": {\
            "value": "Submit"\
          }\
        },\
        "events": {\
          "onClick": [\
            {\
              "name": "validate",\
              "type": "common"\
            },\
            {\
              "name": "logValue",\
              "type": "code"\
            }\
          ]\
        },\
        "tooltipProps": {\
          "text": {\
            "value": "Hello, world!"\
          },\
          "trigger": {\
            "value": [\
              "hover",\
              "focus"\
            ]\
          }\
        }\
      }\
    ]
  },
  "localization": {},
  "languages": [\
    {\
      "code": "en",\
      "dialect": "US",\
      "name": "English",\
      "description": "American English",\
      "bidi": "ltr"\
    }\
  ],
  "defaultLanguage": "en-US"
}
```

The form is represented by the [PersistedForm](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/core/interfaces/PersistedForm/) type. Let's take a closer
look at the parts of this JSON.

### Version [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#version "Direct link to Version")

This is the version of the form saved in JSON format. The list of possible versions is listed
in [PersistedFormVersion](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/core/enumerations/PersistedFormVersion/).

```json
{
  "version": "1",
  ...
}
```

### Actions [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#actions "Direct link to Actions")

A list of actions is simply a Record, the key in that Record is the name of the action, the value is the function body and function
parameters.

```json
{
  "actions": {
    "logValue": {
      "body": "  console.log('Name', e.data.name)",
      "params": {}
    }
  },
  ...
}
```

### Types of error, modal and tooltip components [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#types-of-error-modal-and-tooltip-components "Direct link to Types of error, modal and tooltip components")

- `tooltipType` \- the name of the component type (usually the displayName of the React component) that displays the tooltip.
- `errorType` \- the name of the component type (usually the displayName of the React component) that displays the error.
- `modalType` \- the name of the component type (usually the displayName of the React component) that displays the modal window.

```json
{
  ...
  "tooltipType": "RsTooltip",
  "errorType": "RsErrorMessage",
  "modalType": "RsModal",
  ...
}
```

### Form [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#form "Direct link to Form")

This describes the form itself, which is represented by a component tree, see
the [ComponentStore](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/core/classes/ComponentStore/) type for more details. form is the root element of the
component tree.

```json
{
  ...,
  "form": {
    "key": "Screen",
    "type": "Screen",
    "props": {},
    "children": [\
      {\
        "key": "name",\
        "type": "RsInput",\
        "props": {\
          "label": {\
            "value": "Name"\
          }\
        },\
        "schema": {\
          "validations": [\
            {\
              "key": "required"\
            },\
            {\
              "key": "min",\
              "args": {\
                "limit": 3\
              }\
            }\
          ]\
        }\
      },\
      {\
        "key": "RsButton 1",\
        "type": "RsButton",\
        "props": {\
          "children": {\
            "value": "Submit"\
          }\
        },\
        "events": {\
          "onClick": [\
            {\
              "name": "validate",\
              "type": "common"\
            },\
            {\
              "name": "logValue",\
              "type": "code"\
            }\
          ]\
        },\
        "tooltipProps": {\
          "text": {\
            "value": "Hello, world!"\
          },\
          "trigger": {\
            "value": [\
              "hover",\
              "focus"\
            ]\
          }\
        }\
      }\
    ]
  },
  ...
}
```

### Localization [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#localization "Direct link to Localization")

The next set of data relates to localization.

- `localization` \- a form localization data (
see [LocalizationValue](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/core/type-aliases/LocalizationValue/)).
The [Fluent](https://projectfluent.org/) language is used for localization.
- `languages` \- an array of languages that the form supports. The language is described by
the [Language](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/core/classes/Language/) type.
- `defaultLanguage` \- the default form language, e.g. 'en-US'.

```json
{
  ...
  "localization": {},
  "languages": [\
    {\
      "code": "en",\
      "dialect": "US",\
      "name": "English",\
      "description": "American English",\
      "bidi": "ltr"\
    }\
  ],
  "defaultLanguage": "en-US"
}
```

## JSON schema [​](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/\#json-schema "Direct link to JSON schema")

You have the option to generate a JSON schema for your set of components used in the form. After generating the schema, you can connect it
to your code editor and validate the JSON form using the IDE. Generation is performed using the
[createSchema](https://formengine.io/documentation/api-reference/@react-form-builder/json-schema-generator/functions/createSchema/) function from the
[@react-form-builder/json-schema-generator](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@react-form-builder/json-schema-generator) package.

```typescript
import {builderViewWithCss, components, rSuiteComponentsDescriptions} from '@react-form-builder/components-rsuite'
import {createSchema} from '@react-form-builder/json-schema-generator'

function generateJsonFormSchema() {
  // or use your components
  const myComponents = components
  // or use your component descriptions
  const myDescriptions = rSuiteComponentsDescriptions
  const schema = createSchema(myComponents, [myDescriptions])
  return JSON.stringify(schema, undefined, 2)
}
```

![Form's JSON 07](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-07-dff103d42f74bfc70ec5d7dff362ae44.png)

You can also enable JSON schema generation from the Designer:

```tsx
import {builderViewWithCss} from '@react-form-builder/components-rsuite'
import type {GenerateJsonFormSchemaOptions} from '@react-form-builder/designer'
import {FormBuilder} from '@react-form-builder/designer'
import {createSchema} from '@react-form-builder/json-schema-generator'

const generateJsonFormSchema = (options: GenerateJsonFormSchemaOptions) => {
  const {components, descriptions} = options
  const schema = createSchema(components, descriptions)
  return JSON.stringify(schema, undefined, 2)
}

export const App = () => {
  return <FormBuilder
    view={builderViewWithCss}
    generateJsonFormSchema={generateJsonFormSchema}
  />
}
```

After that, the menu item for downloading JSON schema will be available.

![Form's JSON 05](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-05-d70317d4bb29edbb3f1e819dde892c6b.png)

The built-in JSON form editor will also use a JSON schema:

![Form's JSON 06](https://formengine.io/documentation/assets/images/forms-json-06-e93f9b063d050212a008eb44c8086a9d.png)

- [Export form to JSON](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#export-form-to-json)
- [Get the form's JSON in React component](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#get-the-forms-json-in-react-component)
- [Import form from JSON](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#import-form-from-json)
- [Embedded JSON form editor](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#embedded-json-form-editor)
- [JSON form description](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#json-form-description)
  - [Version](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#version)
  - [Actions](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#actions)
  - [Types of error, modal and tooltip components](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#types-of-error-modal-and-tooltip-components)
  - [Form](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#form)
  - [Localization](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#localization)
- [JSON schema](https://formengine.io/documentation/formengine-designer/features/forms-json/#json-schema)
